DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MARTIN LALOR, MARY LALOR, THE LALOR TRUST UDT DATED DECEMBER 12, 1996'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS DEMANDED IN DEPOSITION, TO RESTRAIN PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL FROM DISRUPTING DEPOSITION, AND FOR SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS TO PRODUCE COMCAST BILL, UTILITY BILLS, AND A COPY OF EMAIL TO THE USPS REGARDING A CHANGE OF ADDRESS. MOTION DENIED AS TO IMMIGRATION INFORMATION AND TAX RETURNS. THE COURT WILL ALLOW 3 HOURS OF DEPOSITION. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion To Compel Resposnes To Deposition Questions, Production Of Documents Demanded In Deposition, To Restrain Pltfs' Counsel From Disrupting Deposition, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARTIN LALOR, MARY LALOR, THE LALOR TRUST UDT DATED DECEMBER 12, 1996'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS DEMANDED IN DEPOSITION, TO RESTRAIN PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL FROM DISRUPTING DEPOSITION, AND FOR SANCTIONS.
Appearances: David Semel of Haapala Thompson & Abern, LLP (510.763.2324) for defendants and moving parties; Philip O'Brien (827.4605) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court ruled as follows: motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is to produce Comcast bill, utility bills, and a copy of email to the USPS regarding a change of address. Motion denied as to immigration information and tax returns. The court will allow 3 hours of deposition. Sanctions are denied. Order signed. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
